app_info.subtitle: Task and project manager
app_info.description: Plate is a lightweight project and task manager built for automation. Connect ChatGPT to your Plate workspace to browse projects and tasks, create and update work items, mark tasks complete, organize work into sections, and leave comments — all without leaving the chat. Plate uses OAuth 2.0 PKCE with Dynamic Client Registration, so no manual API key setup is needed.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves the list of workspaces the authenticated user belongs to and returns their IDs and names without modifying any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Returns data from the user's private Plate account and does not write to or read from any public internet resource.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform any irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves project records from the specified workspace and returns their IDs and names without modifying any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Returns data scoped to the user's private workspace and does not interact with public internet resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform any irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves sections within a project ordered by position and returns their IDs, names, and order without modifying any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Returns data scoped to the user's private workspace and does not interact with public internet resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform any irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only queries and returns tasks from a project with optional filters and does not create, update, or remove any records.
justifications.open_world_justification: Returns data scoped to the user's private workspace and does not interact with public internet resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform any irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only fetches a single task document by ID and returns its fields including description and labels without modifying any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Returns data scoped to the user's private workspace and does not interact with public internet resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform any irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves the workflow statuses configured for a workspace and returns their IDs, names, and colors without modifying any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Returns data scoped to the user's private workspace and does not interact with public internet resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform any irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves workspace member records including userId, name, email, and role without modifying any data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Returns data scoped to the user's private workspace and does not interact with public internet resources.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform any irreversible action.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a new task document in Firestore within the user's private workspace via an atomic transaction that also increments the workspace task counter.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Plate workspace and does not publish content to any public URL or external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new record and does not delete, overwrite, or irreversibly alter existing data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Updates one or more fields on an existing task document in the user's private workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Plate workspace and does not publish content to any public URL or external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Overwrites task fields with new values but does not permanently delete the task or any data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Sets the isCompleted flag and updates the status on an existing task in the user's private workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Plate workspace and does not publish content to any public URL or external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Marks a task complete or re-opens it, which is reversible; it does not delete any data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Permanently removes a task document and its associated comments and attachments from the user's private workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: Deletes data only within the user's private Plate workspace and does not affect any public internet resource.
justifications.destructive_justification: Permanently deletes the task and its associated data from Firestore with no recovery path after the operation completes.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a new project and default section in the user's private workspace, or returns the existing project if one with the same name already exists (findOrCreate). Either way, writes only to the user's private data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Plate workspace and does not publish content to any public URL or external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates new records and does not delete, overwrite, or irreversibly alter existing data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Updates the name or description of an existing project document in the user's private workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Plate workspace and does not publish content to any public URL or external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Overwrites project name or description fields with new values but does not permanently delete the project or any data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a new section in an existing project, or returns the existing section if one with the same name already exists (findOrCreate). Either way, writes only to the user's private data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Plate workspace and does not publish content to any public URL or external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new record and does not delete, overwrite, or irreversibly alter existing data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Updates the name of an existing section document in the user's private workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Plate workspace and does not publish content to any public URL or external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Overwrites the section name with a new value but does not permanently delete the section or any data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a new comment document on a task in the user's private workspace, posted as the authenticated user.
justifications.open_world_justification: Writes only to the user's private Plate workspace and does not publish content to any public URL or external system.
justifications.destructive_justification: Creates a new record and does not delete, overwrite, or irreversibly alter existing data.

justifications.read_only_justification: Permanently removes a comment document from Firestore in the user's private workspace.
justifications.open_world_justification: Deletes data only within the user's private Plate workspace and does not affect any public internet resource.
justifications.destructive_justification: Permanently deletes the comment from Firestore with no recovery path after the operation completes.

test_cases.description: User lists all workspaces they belong to.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a list of workspaces with their names and IDs so the user can identify which workspace to work in.

test_cases.description: User lists open tasks in a project filtered by section.
test_cases.user_prompt: What tasks are in the To Do section of my Backend project?
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the open tasks in the specified section with their names, statuses, and assignees.

test_cases.description: User creates a new task with a specific assignee and status.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a task called 'Fix login bug' in the To Do section of the Backend project and assign it to Jane.
test_cases.tools_triggered: list_workspaces, list_projects, list_sections, list_statuses, list_members, create_task
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the new task ID and auto-assigned task number confirming the task was created.

test_cases.description: User marks a task as complete.
test_cases.user_prompt: Mark the 'Fix login bug' task as done.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the task ID and isCompleted: true confirming the task is marked complete and its status updated.

test_cases.description: User gets full details of a specific task including its description.
test_cases.user_prompt: What's the description of task SCA-42?
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the task's full details including its rich-text description content.

test_cases.description: User creates a new project with a description.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a project called 'Q3 Launch' in my workspace.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the project ID, the default section ID, and created: true if a new project was created, or created: false if a project with that name already existed.

test_cases.description: User adds a comment to a task.
test_cases.user_prompt: Add a comment to the 'Fix login bug' task: 'Reproduced on Safari, investigating.'
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the new comment ID confirming the comment was posted to the task.

negative_test_cases.description: User asks about calendar or scheduling, which Plate does not support.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What meetings do I have tomorrow?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because Plate manages tasks and projects, not calendar events or meetings.

negative_test_cases.description: User asks to send an email, which is outside Plate's scope.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Send an email to my team about the sprint review.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because Plate does not send emails or messages to external recipients.

negative_test_cases.description: User asks to write or run code, which Plate does not support.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Write a Python script to parse this CSV file.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because Plate manages tasks and projects, not code execution or file processing.
